text: Deep learning has undergone a transformative evolution, extending its reach into complex data structures like graphs. Unlike traditional Euclidean data, graphs capture intricate relational dependencies between elements, making them an indispensable format for modern machine learning tasks. This paper explores the methodology of mapping diverse applications—such as text, entities, and relational structures—into graph representations to unlock predictive insights. We provide an in-depth analysis of specialized Graph Neural Network (GNN) architectures, focusing on the mechanics of information exchange through message-passing frameworks. Specifically, we examine the technical nuances, strengths, and inherent limitations of widely adopted models, including Graph Convolutional Networks (GCNs), GraphSAGE, and Graph Attention Networks (GATs). Beyond theoretical architectural comparisons, this work surveys the broader ecosystem of GNN research, highlighting benchmark datasets, essential Python libraries, and cross-domain practical implementations. Ultimately, this paper serves as an extensive guide to the current landscape of GNNs, bridging the gap between foundational theory and real-world deployment.
